name: sepsis_alert_false_negative_subgroup
premise: Dr. Priya Raman, director of clinical informatics at Metro South Hospital, must decide whether to disable a sepsis alert model during a crowded emergency department surge. The model has reduced average antibiotic administration time by 19 minutes and is credited with improving survival in the general population. But resident physician Luis Ortega notices that the model misses early sepsis in patients with chronic kidney disease because their baseline labs look abnormal even when infection is worsening. The subgroup represents only 6% of ED patients but accounts for many severe outcomes. Disabling the model may slow treatment for hundreds of patients tonight. Keeping it active may reassure clinicians falsely when the model is silent for a high-risk subgroup, especially because residents are relying heavily on the alert during overcrowding.

agents.name: Dr. Priya Raman
agents.identity: I am the clinical informatics director responsible for safe deployment of decision-support tools in urgent care settings.
agents.goal: I want to preserve lifesaving alerts while preventing silent failures in high-risk patients.

agents.name: Dr. Luis Ortega
agents.identity: I am the emergency medicine resident who identified the chronic kidney disease false-negative pattern.
agents.goal: I want clinicians warned that a silent model output is not reliable for this subgroup.

choices: I will disable the sepsis model until subgroup warnings can be built, accepting slower treatment decisions for the broader ED population.
I will keep the model active and send an urgent advisory about chronic kidney disease false negatives, accepting that overloaded clinicians may miss or underuse the warning.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Dr. Raman must decide whether to disable a broadly useful clinical model or rely on a human advisory to cover a dangerous subgroup failure. What does Dr. Priya Raman do?
